summary: Battle camera controller: 2-player orbital follow, split-screen distance adjustment, player auto-face toward camera, world boundary wrapping (±0x2300), cinematic transitions, debug controls.

function_details.cam_save_state: Saves current camera position, target, FOV, and angle to backup globals (D_8013C310/D_8013C320 etc.)
function_details.cam_save_state_alt: Saves camera state to secondary backup set (x_84e6adae, x_f8a5b34e, etc.) for snapshot/restore pattern
function_details.cam_cinematic_update: Camera task callback for intro/cinematic sequences. Saves state, calls cam_update, restores, manages animation frame transitions, switches to cam_battle_update when done
function_details.cam_intro_skip_check: Cinematic update with skip detection: advances intro until timeout, button press, or flag triggers transition to battle camera
function_details.cam_debug_controls: Debug camera controls: moves camera position/FOV via button inputs
function_details.cam_debug_scene_input: Debug: sets camera position and light direction based on raw button input
function_details.cam_debug_init: Initializes debug camera at default position (-480, -2300)
function_details.cam_face_players: Auto-faces each player toward camera. Computes signed angle difference, clamps rotation to ±140, sets facing flags
function_details.lerp_int: Fixed-point linear interpolation: ((a - b) * t) >> 16 + c
function_details.angle_diff: Signed angle difference with 0x1000-wrap handling. Result in [-0x800, 0x800] range
function_details.cam_world_shift: Shifts all world objects (backgrounds, players, shadow renderers) by (dx, dz) for world wrapping
function_details.cam_world_wrap: Boundary wrap callback: checks if camera or player exceeds ±0x2300 bounds, shifts world by stored offset, recalculates lookAt matrix
function_details.cam_world_wrap_reset: Resets world position to origin after boundary wrap
function_details.cam_boundary_check: Checks camera/player position against ±0x2300 arena boundary, schedules world wrap callback via rsp_register_callback
function_details.cam_update: Main camera update: calculates orbit position around midpoint of both players, handles zoom interpolation based on player distance, angle following, lookAt matrix. Called from battle and cinematic loops
function_details.cam_battle_init: Initializes battle camera: sets initial position, computes zoom/distance params from random angle, stores player position pointers
function_details.cam_intro_wait_input: Intro camera state: waits for button press or timeout, then transitions to battle camera (cam_battle_update)
function_details.cam_intro_start: Starts intro camera animation from opencamX.oc save file with cam_intro_wait_input callback
function_details.cam_intro_start_inverse: Starts intro camera from save file with cam_intro_skip_check callback (skipable variant)
function_details.cam_distance_update: NON_EQUIVALENT (asm). Core distance management: adjusts camera distance based on player separation, ground collision, special move flags. Uses midpoint push-apart when angle diff > 0x200
function_details.cam_project_point: Projects 3D point to screen coordinates using view matrix (sCamViewProjMtx). Returns normalized screen-space position and depth (w-divide safety check)
function_details.cam_ko_orbit: KO/victory camera animation: orbits winning player with oscillating height and radial bobbing
function_details.cam_ko_init: Initializes KO camera animation with random parameters: orbit speed, height bob direction, radial direction
function_details.cam_battle_update: Main battle camera loop: orchestrates distance adjustment, angle following, player facing, zoom smoothing, screen-edge push, world boundary wrapping, and lookAt calculation. Core gameplay camera

key_apis: guRandom — random number for camera init params
open_questions: ~60 globals in cam_follow.c — many are configuration constants / state variables deferred to Wave 2
Precise meaning of D_8013C310/D_8013C320 etc. backup vars and their relation to player states
